chunks.title: CSCE 638 Syllabus
chunks.content:
Section 600 (54988)
Natural Language Processing: Foundations and Techniques
Spring 2026 - College Station

Catalog Description
Natural Language Processing: Foundations and Techniques. (3-0). Credit 3. Focus on teaching Natural Language Processing (NLP) fundamentals including language models, automatic syntactic processing and semantic understanding; introduction to major NLP applications including information extraction, machine translation, text summarization, dialogue systems and sentiment analysis.

Special Course Designation
None

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

*   Identify and explain the key concepts and models the fundamental concepts and models for natural language processing.
*   Be aware of recent trends in natural language processing research.
*   Apply established techniques and propose practical methods and solutions to tackle challenges in natural language processing.

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
This material Is: Recommended
Speech and Language Processing. An Introduction to Natural Language Processing, Computational Linguistics, and Speech Recognition
ISBN: 9791221476842
Authors: Daniel Jurafsky, James H. Martin
Publication Date: 2023-01-01

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Assignments 31%
*   Assignment 0 1%
*   Assignment 1 10%
*   Assignment 2 10%
*   Assignment 3 10%
Quizzes 40%
*   Quiz 1 10%
*   Quiz 2 10%
*   Quiz 3 10%
*   Quiz 4 10%
Course Project 26%
*   Project Proposal 3%
*   Project Presentation 10%
*   Project Report 13%
Participation 3%
The grading scale will be:
*   A = 90-100
*   B = 80-89
*   C = 70-79
*   D = 60-69
*   F = <60

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

Course Specific Late Work Policy
*   Assignment 1-3, Project Proposal, Project Report
    *   1 day late: 10% penalty
    *   2 days late: 20% penalty
    *   3 days late: 30% penalty
    *   4 days late: 50% penalty
    *   5 or more days late: 100% penalty
*   Assignment 0, Project Presentation
    *   No late submissions allowed

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
| Week | Topics |
|---|---|
| 1 | Overview and Machine Learning Basics |
| 2 | Word Representations |
| 3 | Language Modeling, Convolutional Neural Network, Recurrent Neural Network |
| 4 | Sequential Labeling, Sequence-to-Sequence, Attention |
| 5 | Transformers |
| 6 | Contextualized Representations, Pre-Training, Large Language Models |
| 7 | Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning, Model Distillation, Evaluation |
| 8 | Instruction Tuning, Alignment, Post-Training |
| 9 | Large Reasoning Models, Test-Time Scaling, Decoding |
| 10 | Text Similarity, Retrieval-Augmented Generation, Agents |
| 11 | Multilingual Language Models, Vision-Language Models |
| 12 | Adversarial Attack and Defense, AI-Generated Text Detection |
| 13 | Non-Autoregressive Generation |
| 14 | Bias Mitigation, Hallucinations |
| 15 | Project Presentation |

Important Dates
Assignments
*   Assignment 0: 1/29
*   Assignment 1: 2/10
*   Assignment 2: 3/3
*   Assignment 3: 3/24
Quizzes
*   Quiz 1: 2/4
*   Quiz 2: 2/25
*   Quiz 3: 3/18
*   Quiz 4: 4/13
Course Project
*   Project Proposal: 3/6
*   Project Presentation: 4/20, 4/22, 4/27
*   Project Report: 4/30
